evidence: QueryExecutor interface has exactly one implementation (BlackLabQueryExecutor), but it deliberately isolates the BlackLab external library dependency — this is an intentional adapter boundary, not gratuitous abstraction.

WordSketchApiServer.registerGetHandler and registerPostHandler are nearly identical — each creates a context, handles OPTIONS, checks method (GET vs POST), and dispatches. The only difference is the method string. This duplication could be collapsed into a single registerHandler(path, method, handler) method.

handleFullSketchForType in SketchHandlers uses a boolean isDep flag to branch into two different response-building paths (one builds relData with deprel/description, the other with cql/collocate_pos_group). This boolean-dispatching pattern signals two responsibilities in one method.
RelationUtils is a 35-line class with one real method (resolveRelationAlias) backed by a static Map.of(). Minimal overhead, purposeful use.

ExplorationHandlers.resolveRelationConfig, resolveExploreParams, parseSeedSet, buildBaseExploreResponse, buildExploreResponseBody are all private helpers that do genuine work — not pass-throughs. Good extraction.

PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate and RelationConfig.getFullPattern(String, String) cover the same conceptual operation (headword+collocate substitution) via overlapping paths. RelationConfig delegates CQL walking to CqlUtils; PatternSubstitution does the same CqlUtils walk independently. Partial overlap.
CollocateQueryHelper is a package-private class (not an interface), so its abstraction overhead is zero — it adds shared logic without adding an unnecessary interface layer.
ExploreOptions record cleanly bundles 5 exploration parameters that were previously spread across method signatures — this abstraction pays for itself.

strengths: CollocateQueryHelper is package-private (no interface), adding shared logic at zero abstraction overhead.
ExploreOptions record reduces a 5-parameter call to a single structured value that self-documents its fields.
RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias centralizes alias resolution in one place, preventing drift across handlers.
ExplorationHandlers private helpers (resolveRelationConfig, resolveExploreParams) do genuine parsing and validation work, not mere pass-throughs.
PatternSubstitution and CqlUtils separation is coherent: CqlUtils handles parsing primitives; PatternSubstitution handles the substitution operation.
abstraction_fitness.issue_character: The abstraction issues are localized and structural rather than systemic: a boolean-flag dispatch in SketchHandlers signals an imprecise boundary, and two near-identical HTTP registration helpers are not collapsed. These are code smell-level issues, not architectural problems.
abstraction_fitness.score_rationale: The codebase's abstractions largely pay for themselves. Most classes are cohesive and purpose-built. The QueryExecutor interface earns its place as an isolation adapter. The main drag comes from the isDep boolean dispatch (two responsibilities in one method) and the registerGetHandler/registerPostHandler duplication (two nearly-identical 8-line methods). At the global anchor of 80 = solid but uneven, this codebase is slightly below that: mostly solid with a few abstraction choices that add indirection or overlap without full payoff.

issues.summary: registerGetHandler and registerPostHandler are near-identical and should be collapsed into a shared helper
evidence: registerGetHandler and registerPostHandler differ only in the method string (GET vs POST) and the requireMethod check; both create a context, handle OPTIONS with the same pattern, delegate to handler.handle(exchange), and share all other logic.
issues.suggestion: Extract a private registerEndpoint(HttpServer, String path, String method, HttpHandler handler) method; call it from both registerGetHandler and registerPostHandler.

issues.summary: handleFullSketchForType uses a boolean isDep flag to produce two different response shapes, masking a genuine boundary
evidence: handleFullSketchForType branches on boolean isDep to decide between buildRelationResponse (deprel/description/total_matches) and an inline Map with cql/collocate_pos_group fields — two incompatible response shapes in one method.
The extracted buildRelationResponse static helper only covers the isDep=true path, making the isDep=false path inline but the isDep=true path extracted — asymmetric extraction.
issues.suggestion: Split into two methods: buildSurfaceRelationEntry and buildDepRelationEntry, each responsible for one response shape. Call the appropriate one from the respective surface/dep sketch handlers.

root_causes: The boolean-dispatch and handler-duplication patterns both stem from incremental feature addition (surface sketch → dep sketch → unified handler) without a subsequent cleanup pass.
